_provenance.method: Barrier state model: van Genuchten θ(h) applied to skin barrier permeability (Paper 12 §2.3)
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Exact matches use 1e-10 for barrier/breach/d_eff; qualitative checks use expected ranges for VG retention and conductivity.
references: Paper 12 §2.3: Dimensional promotion via barrier disruption
Tagami H (2008) Br J Dermatol 158:431-436 (TEWL as barrier metric)
Fluhr JW et al. (2006) Exp Dermatol 15:483-492 (skin barrier biophysics)
notes: Exp 068: Barrier state model — skin as porous medium
VG parameters repurposed: θ_r=min barrier, θ_s=max barrier, α=permeability, n=pore geometry
TEWL (transepidermal water loss) maps to K(h) — higher TEWL = more permeable = breached barrier
Breach fraction maps to effective Anderson dimension d_eff = 2 + f

barrier_vg_retention.description: Skin barrier integrity modeled as VG retention curve: barrier_integrity(stress) ~ θ(h)

test_cases.label: Intact skin (no stress h=0) → full barrier

barrier_conductivity.description: Cytokine permeability through barrier modeled as VG K(h): higher K = more transport

test_cases.label: Intact barrier (h=0) → max K = max potential transport
Stressed barrier → K decreases (less barrier = paradoxically less cytokine RETENTION)
Very dry (breached) → K very low (barrier structure gone, transport through disrupted channels)

barrier_to_d_eff_mapping.description: Normalized barrier integrity → breach fraction → d_eff (2D→3D promotion)

skin_vg_params.description: Published skin VG-analogue parameters for different barrier states

test_cases.label: Healthy human skin (thick epidermis)
test_cases._note: Low α = low permeability = strong barrier; human epidermis 50-100μm

test_cases.label: Healthy canine skin (thin epidermis)
test_cases._note: Higher α = more permeable = thinner barrier; canine epidermis 20-30μm

test_cases.label: AD-affected skin (disrupted barrier)
test_cases._note: Much higher α = highly permeable; n close to 1 = broad pore distribution
